Drug Pricing Transparency

G
Pharmacy Acquisition Cost (NADAC)
$1.98 per unit
Medicare Part D Cost per Unit
$3.16 per unit
Markup Ratio (Part D / NADAC)
1.60x

Medicare Part D cost is moderately above pharmacy acquisition cost, reflecting typical distribution and dispensing margins.

NADAC = National Average Drug Acquisition Cost, reflecting what pharmacies pay wholesalers. Source: CMS NADAC (12/18/2024).

Methodology

Data source: Medicare Part D Spending by Drug (dataset ID: partd-drug-spending). Vintage: 2023. Downloaded: 2026-04-13. Rows in source: 14,309.

Medicare Part D Spending by Drug reports total spending, claims, and beneficiaries for prescription drugs under the Medicare Part D program. Part B spending data covers drugs administered by physicians. Discarded units data shows wastage of single-dose vial drugs.

Full methodology →
